title: Three futures for Africa's clinical trial landscape: scenario projections to 2035
type: cross-sectional registry analysis with scenario projection
primary_estimand: Projected annual trial counts under three scenarios and years to Latin American per-capita parity
certainty: moderate (registry data for baseline, model-dependent projections)
body: What will Africa's clinical trial landscape look like in 2035 under different growth assumptions? We extracted temporal trial data from ClinicalTrials.gov API v2 for Uganda from 1998 to 2026 and modelled three scenarios: status quo linear extrapolation of 2019-2025 trends, optimistic growth matching India's 15 percent annual rate, and pessimistic decline of 5 percent per year reflecting PEPFAR cuts and post-COVID stagnation. Under status quo assumptions, Uganda's annual trial output would grow modestly, while under the optimistic scenario it would increase several-fold, and under pessimistic assumptions it would decline toward single digits by 2035. The years-to-parity calculation, measuring time until Uganda matches Latin America's current per-capita trial rate, showed that even the optimistic scenario requires over two decades while parity is unreachable under pessimistic assumptions. The evidence gap between Africa's current trial rate and the Latin American benchmark translates to thousands of missing trials per year, each representing clinical questions unanswered for African populations. Closing this gap requires simultaneous investment in domestic R and D spending, regulatory harmonization through the African Medicines Agency, NCD research rebalancing, and Phase 1 infrastructure development.
ai_transparency: LLM assistance was used in early drafting. The final text was rewritten by the author, who takes full responsibility for the content.

checks.name: primary estimate in sentences 3-4
checks.detail: Sentence 3 reports the three scenario outputs and sentence 4 reports years-to-parity.

checks.name: boundary sentence present
checks.detail: Sentence 7 identifies the investment requirements for closing the gap.
